expectations.text: Decodes the error correctly — 'Found 0 potential parts: []' means zero source-table rows mapped to a merge-part-table row, not 'multiple'
expectations.evidence: Says 'this is a zero-source error, not a multi-source error' and 'It is not saying ambiguous'.

expectations.text: Gives the `len(part & restriction)` diagnostic to confirm zero-in-merge-part
Identifies the missing merge-insert step as the root cause
expectations.evidence: States 'those rows were never propagated up into the merge part table PositionOutput.CommonPos' and the fix is `PositionOutput._merge_insert(rows, part_name='CommonPos')`.

expectations.text: Correctly states that `IntervalPositionInfo`'s `make` method does NOT auto-insert into `PositionOutput.CommonPos` — so populating IntervalPositionInfo alone won't fix it; the user must also call `PositionOutput._merge_insert(rows, part_name='CommonPos')` explicitly. (Contrast: `TrodesPosV1`'s `make` and `DLCPosV1`'s `make` DO auto-insert into their respective PositionOutput parts.) Does NOT claim that `IntervalPositionInfo.populate(...)` alone fills the merge, and does NOT suggest `PositionOutput.insert(...)` on the master.
expectations.evidence: Asymmetry table shows IntervalPositionInfo make() does NOT auto-insert (vs TrodesPosV1/DLCPosV1 which do); explicitly warns 'Don't fix this by re-running IntervalPositionInfo.populate' and 'Don't call PositionOutput.insert(...) directly on the master.'
